dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (nih gift-offering register)
notes: Sentence-initial 'nih' on a gift-giving moment — 'untukmu' = 'for you' (intimate 2nd-person possessive). NMT typically expands 'untukmu' as 'this is for you' to fill the English clause shape. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'Here, for you' is more direct (literal); 'Here, this is for you' adds the deictic 'this' which the source doesn't have explicitly — could be wrong if the partner pair would say it more tersely.
